title: All About Me and My Family Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The people who live with you and love you.
rounds.explanation: family: The people who live with you and love you.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: An older person, or someone who has learned more.
rounds.explanation: senior: An older person, or someone who has learned more.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Something you must do or take care of.
rounds.explanation: duty: Something you must do or take care of.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A mother or a father who looks after you.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To act in a good and kind way.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: How well your body and your mind feel.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My family has my mum, my dad, my sister, and me.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My friend Nora and I play football after school.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My little brother is a junior in the school choir.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from “Good Behaviour at School”: At school, we show ______ to our teacher and to our friends.
rounds.explanation: The text opens by naming what we show to our teacher and friends, and that word is respect.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from “Amal's Big Day”: She whispered to her best friend, Nora, “What is your ______ in the play?”
rounds.explanation: Amal asks what job Nora must take care of in the play, and a job you must do is a duty.

rounds.prompt: Where does “Public and Private” say you can play games and be loud?
choices: In public, at the busy market in town.
In the lunch area at school.
At the school gate in the morning.
rounds.explanation: The listening text pairs in private with at home, and every other option names a public place.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What does Amal say her duty at home is?
choices: Her duty is to keep her room tidy and to be kind.
Her parents are her role models, and they teach her to help other people.
Because she is the youngest member of the drama club.
rounds.answer: Her duty is to keep her room tidy and to be kind.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Her duty is to keep her room tidy and to be kind.

rounds.prompt: Amal talks about her grandmother. Should she say he or she about her? Why?
choices: Her parents are her role models, and they teach her to help other people.
She runs over at once and offers to help the younger girl.
She should say she, because grandmother names one female person.
rounds.answer: She should say she, because grandmother names one female person.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She should say she, because grandmother names one female person.

rounds.prompt: Why do the drama club students call Amal Junior?
choices: She is acting the senior student, so Amal can show the polite way.
Because she is the youngest member of the drama club.
She should say she, because grandmother names one female person.
rounds.answer: Because she is the youngest member of the drama club.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Because she is the youngest member of the drama club.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Introduce Your Family
rounds.target: In my family, there is ______, ______, and me.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 — Ask Me a Question
Speaking 5 — Public or Private Role Play
rounds.target: Act out two short scenes with a partner or your helper.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: His good behaviour made the whole class happy.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: We honour our parents by helping them at home.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: We hold hands near the road for our safety.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'behave' mean in this unit?
choices: To act in a good and kind way.
The people who live with you and love you.
A mother or a father who looks after you.
rounds.answer: To act in a good and kind way.
rounds.explanation: behave: To act in a good and kind way.

rounds.prompt: What does 'health' mean in this unit?
choices: The people who live with you and love you.
A mother or a father who looks after you.
How well your body and your mind feel.
rounds.answer: How well your body and your mind feel.
rounds.explanation: health: How well your body and your mind feel.

rounds.prompt: What does 'parent' mean in this unit?
choices: A person you like and spend time with.
A mother or a father who looks after you.
The people who live with you and love you.
rounds.answer: A mother or a father who looks after you.
rounds.explanation: parent: A mother or a father who looks after you.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'respect'?
rounds.explanation: respect is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'private'?
rounds.explanation: private is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'student'?
rounds.explanation: student is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A person you like and spend time with.
A person who goes to school to learn new things.
An older person, or someone who has learned more.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The way a person acts towards other people.
Being kind to people and listening when they speak.
Something you must do or take care of.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Being safe and not in danger.
Just for you or a small group, not for everyone.
The people who live with you and love you.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which Wh- word asks about a time?
rounds.explanation: When opens a time answer, while who opens a person and where opens a place.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence gives a clear instruction to a friend?
rounds.explanation: An imperative starts with the base verb and leaves out you, so Listen to your teacher is correct.

rounds.prompt: Complete this sentence: Nora ______ reading books at night.
rounds.explanation: Nora is one person, so she takes likes with an s, just as he and it do.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Amal lives with her parents, her brothers and her grandmother. What do we call this group of people?
rounds.explanation: A family is the people who live with you and love you, so the group in the house is her family.

rounds.prompt: In the class play, Nora acts as the older student who has learned more. What is she called?
rounds.explanation: A senior is an older person or someone who has learned more, which is exactly the part Nora plays.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the present continuous correctly?
choices: The students are practising their lines.
The students is practising their lines.
The students practise their lines last week.
rounds.answer: The students are practising their lines.
rounds.explanation: Present continuous needs a be word plus the -ing verb, and a plural subject takes are practising.
